meta.title: IRB Briefing for Juliann Allen & Jon Murphy
meta.purpose: Pre-meeting summary of studies proposed for review

studies.name: Conjoint Analysis
studies.subtitle: Protocol Modification
studies.request: Add the Loss Aversion Inventory (LAI) to the existing protocol.
studies.change: Minimal — one additional measure; no change to core procedures or risk level.

studies.name: Emotional Intelligence & Fluid Intelligence Study
studies.subtitle: New study

fields.label: Purpose
fields.value: Test whether fluid EI is distinct from crystallized IQ

studies.procedures: Self-estimates → video-based EI assessment → Raven's Progressive Matrices → immediate feedback (percentiles, bias visualization, radar charts).
studies.note: Feedback reports will be provided for review before approval.

fields.label: Purpose
fields.value: Compare student vs. HR professional ratings of HR tactics across 27 scenarios

fields.label: Participants
fields.value: MNGT 425 students + HR professionals (recruited via networks)

fields.label: Purpose
fields.value: Compare interests, values, skills, personality to 867 O*NET occupations

studies.name: Goal Setting (A Study in Decision Making)
studies.subtitle: Approved

fields.label: Purpose
fields.value: Replication of Schweitzer et al. (2004) — goal setting and unethical behavior

issues.issue: Minimal risk; anonymized data (PRAMS IDs only); electronic consent
HR professionals enter first/last name for ID generation; names not stored with research data
Feedback includes Dunning–Kruger–style bias comparison; debrief materials available for review
Lab study; anagram tasks; optional cash reward (up to $14) per approved protocol

federal_audit.context: SONA/PRAMS is live but shadow IT. We will not use course credit or monitor bonus points. What is required to survive a federal audit?
federal_audit.why_no_credit: Without credit/bonus, participation is voluntary research—not used for grading. Per Cornell IRB and case law (Owasso v. Falvo, Hardin County Schools v. Foster), voluntary research data not used for institutional decisions is not FERPA-protected education records.

items: Risk minimization; data security (TLS, access controls)
Keep research data anonymous or de-identified
No link between participation and grades
Audit logging for protocol submissions, IRB decisions, exports
Log data exports; log AI API calls (hash only)
Purge or anonymize when no longer needed

disable_without_credit: Student ID for research participants — don't collect
recommended_minimum: Disable credit allocation and bonus-point features
Use anonymous participation (session_id, no participant FK)
Document that participation is voluntary and not used for grades
Maintain IRB approval for all studies
Add audit logging for protocol submissions, IRB decisions, exports
Restrict self-registration to participant role only
Retain consent records and protocol documentation per IRB
